page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, eliminating simultaneous multithreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity is rated at a 17 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark testing, the m9gd.8xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. PassMark memory and database operations benchmarks also register in the top tier, supported by strong cached read-only memory bandwidth. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations perform in the top 25%, while single-core stress-ng and uncached memory bandwidth write tests fall into the average range.

The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated ARMv9 cores offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring low-latency local storage. However, the lack of AVX/AMX instruction sets and SMT may limit performance for legacy x86-optimized software or highly threaded floating-point tasks. This instance is suited for database operations, static web serving, caching layers, and medium-scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128 GB of RAM, a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the arm64 architecture provides dedicated physical cores without simultaneous multithreading. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded compression, alongside strong multi-core processing. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its high local storage capacity and memory performance relative to its ARM-based hardware class. It is suited for database hosting, web serving, and caching workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.

tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, integrated NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier database and compression performance.

128 GB of system memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database hosting, caching layers, and web serving
